title: Specific version tag for image should be preferred
description: While using always the latest version may seem convenient, the build cannot be repeated because it is not clear which was the last version. In addition, it can lead to unpredictability and issues such as version mismatch and potential security vulnerabilities. What is the potential impact? For example, if a developer builds and deploys an application using my-image:latest, they may unknowingly be using a different version of the image than another developer who also built and deployed the same application using my-image:latest.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Specific version tag for image should be preferred. Using generic tags like 'latest', 'stable', or no tag leads to unpredictable builds and potential version mismatches. Specify an exact version tag (e.g., ubuntu:20.04, node:18.16.0) for reproducible and reliable builds.
